context_values.response.expand_spec:
The spec already exists at `apps/solitaire/spec.md` and is comprehensive. It covers all six areas you requested:

| Requested Topic | Spec Coverage |
|---|---|
| **Game rules & data structures** | ¬ß3 (Rules of Play), ¬ß4 (Core Data Model) ‚Äî Card, Deck, Suit, all pile types (Stock, Waste, Foundation, Tableau), GameState, Cursor/Selection |
| **Terminal rendering (curses)** | ¬ß6 (Terminal Rendering Approach) ‚Äî `curses.wrapper`, color pairs, card rendering format, Unicode/ASCII fallback, rendering strategy per pile type |
| **Input handling & move validation** | ¬ß5 (Move Validation Rules), ¬ß8 (Input Handling) ‚Äî legal sources/destinations, validation helpers, atomic moves, key map, state machine (`NAVIGATE`‚Üí`SELECTED`‚Üí`WON`) |
| **Win/loss detection** | ¬ß9 (Win and Progress Detection) ‚Äî win when foundations total 52 cards, no hard loss state, optional soft "no moves" hint |
| **UI layout** | ¬ß7 (UI Layout Specification) ‚Äî ASCII art mockup, header/top-row/tableau/status zones, 100√ó30 minimum, card-aware cursor for tableau |
| **Test strategy** | ¬ß12 (Test Strategy) ‚Äî unit tests for cards, rules, deals, moves, stock/waste cycle, win detection; property/invariant tests; UI helper tests; manual QA checklist |

The spec also includes additional sections on architecture (¬ß10), error handling/edge cases (¬ß11), acceptance criteria (¬ß13), and future enhancements (¬ß14).

The implementation is fully built out across `src/models.py`, `src/game.py`, `src/ui.py`, and `main.py`, with 44 passing tests. A `review.md` documents the code review findings (all minor). The spec as written guided a complete, working terminal solitaire game ‚Äî no changes needed.
